reasoning: By early November 2025, SpaceX has completed only 5 Starship launches in the calendar year (with Flight 11 taking place in mid-October). To reach the 10-launch threshold, SpaceX would have to conduct 5 additional launches in the remaining 7 weeks of the year. Given that their fastest cadence this year has been roughly one launch every 1-2 months, and hardware availability alongside regulatory/turnaround constraints make 5 rapid-succession launches practically impossible in such a short window, the probability of reaching 10-12 launches successfully reaching space in 2025 is virtually non-existent. I am therefore selecting the minimum permitted bound (0.02).
